headline: Cyril Imhof anchors the cycle on Anthropic's enterprise push, dissecting Claude's Model Context Protocol strategy and its contested path into Microsoft Teams and Slack.
body: Cyril Imhof runs a multi-part thread on Anthropic's Claude Tag and MCP-based agent access, reporting Claude Tag is used in 60% of internal interactions [12] and positioning external agent access through MCPs as a client retention and licensing lever [6][13]. He frames Claude's Teams and Slack integration as an enterprise marketing play [4][10], then argues Microsoft's EU market strength and its competing Copilot product are blocking direct Teams integration, raising anticompetitive questions in the Anthropic versus Microsoft agent battle [7][8]. Alongside this, Imhof flags Chinese models winning adoption on cost and data sovereignty [9] and a rapidly closing gap between open-source and frontier systems [11], while questioning whether general-purpose AI actually beats purpose-built software on long-term efficiency [0]. Cole Medin separately reviews Claude Sonnet 5 as a significant, high-value advance [1].
